summary: The notebook demonstrates a practical inference pipeline for a segmentation competition, focusing on understanding the RLE submission format and establishing a baseline using an 'authentic-only' strategy. It walks through custom RLE encoding, loads two pre-trained models (a custom CNN and a DINOv2-based segmenter), and applies test-time augmentation during inference. Post-processing steps include adaptive thresholding, morphological operations, and confidence/area filtering to generate final masks. The author emphasizes that submitting 'authentic' for all images is a valid starting point for highly imbalanced datasets to quickly validate the submission pipeline and understand the scoring metric.
competition_flow: Loads test images, runs inference with TTA on two pre-trained models, applies adaptive thresholding and morphological post-processing, filters by area and confidence, encodes masks to RLE format, and saves a CSV submission.
data_reading: Reads test images from a local directory using PIL.Image and os.listdir, converts to RGB, and resizes to 512x512.
Loads sample submission CSV with pandas.
data_processing: Resizes images to 512x512 and normalizes pixel values to [0, 1].
Applies test-time augmentation (horizontal flip, vertical flip, 90-degree rotation) during inference.
Applies Gaussian blur, adaptive thresholding (mean + 0.3 * std), and morphological closing/opening operations to post-process predictions.
ensembling: Averages predictions from two models (custom CNN and DINOv2) using a mean strategy, followed by adaptive thresholding, morphological filtering, and area/confidence checks before RLE encoding.
insights: Submitting 'authentic' for all images is a fast, effective baseline for highly imbalanced segmentation tasks to validate pipelines and understand metric behavior.
Test-time augmentation (flips and rotations) reliably improves prediction stability without retraining.
Adaptive thresholding based on local mean and standard deviation outperforms fixed thresholds for post-processing segmentation masks.
